commands.review_summary:
The branch restricts Discord OpenAI agent-proxy wake matching to leading or trailing one- or two-word activation names, adds fuzzy edge matching, tightens wakeNames schema with doctor migration, and updates docs, metadata, tests, and changelog.

PR surface: Source +192, Tests +213, Docs 0, Generated 0. Total +405 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main matches wake names anywhere in the transcript and strips only leading names, while the PR moves matching to edge candidates. I did not run live Discord audio.
commands.review_followup: The protected maintainer label plus compatibility-sensitive wake-name schema and delivery changes require maintainer approval; no narrow automation repair is currently identified.

target.title: fix: tighten Discord voice wake matching
target.body:
## Summary
- Tighten Discord realtime voice wake matching to activation names at the start or end of final transcripts.
- Add generic fuzzy matching for close ASR variants without hardcoded agent names.
- Reject configured wake names over two words or empty lists, and migrate legacy unsupported values with doctor.
- Update Discord voice docs, UI hints, generated channel metadata, tests, and changelog.

## Verification
- `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/doctor.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts -- -t "wake names|reuses recently ignored"`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Discord realtime voice now wakes only on one- or two-word activation names at transcript edges, accepts close ASR variants such as Molty/Monty/Multi, supports trailing activation phrases, and prevents long or empty configured wake-name lists from disabling wake behavior.
Real environment tested: Local Discord voice manager realtime bridge tests.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/voice/manager.e2e.test.ts -- -t "wake names|reuses recently ignored"`
Evidence after fix: 1 test file passed; 6 wake-name tests passed and 110 unrelated tests were skipped by the focused filter.
Observed result after fix: Leading, trailing, fuzzy, configured, fallback, and recently ignored wake-name paths pass; opener-prefixed configured names like "Okay Google" still match at the leading edge.
What was not tested: Live Discord audio room after this patch.

<!-- agent-transcript:start -->
## Agent Transcript

<details>
<summary>Redacted Codex session transcript</summary>

```text
source: local Codex session, sanitized and manually trimmed to this PR scope
redaction: system/developer prompts, raw tool output, secrets, auth/session details, and broad local paths omitted

fix, autoreview, PR, land, include transcripts

[assistant]
Used autoreview and agent-transcript. Finished the empty wake-name review fix first, then planned to rerun proof/review, create the PR with a sanitized transcript block, and land once CI was green.

[assistant]
Checked status. One schema edit was unstaged on top of the staged Discord voice wake-name patch, so the config/doctor side was tightened to prevent empty configured wake-name arrays from silently disabling wake activation.

[assistant]
Added schema/test/doctor coverage: `wakeNames` must be non-empty when configured, and doctor removes legacy empty lists so unset wake names keep the default agent/OpenClaw fallback.

[assistant]
Ran formatting, regenerated bundled channel config metadata, then ran focused tests for Discord config/doctor and realtime wake-name behavior.

[assistant]
Autoreview reported one actionable finding: configured wake names beginning with opener words like `Okay Google` were accepted by schema but stripped before matching. Fixed leading-edge matching to check both raw leading candidates and opener-stripped candidates.

[assistant]
Added regression coverage for opener-prefixed configured wake names, reran focused Discord voice tests, metadata check, and whitespace check.

[assistant]
Final autoreview result: clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported. Committed the staged patch as `fix: tighten Discord voice wake matching`.

[assistant]
Pushed branch `fix/discord-voice-edge-wake-names`, prepared PR body with Summary, Verification, Real behavior proof, and this redacted transcript section.
```

</details>
